title: The Secret.
content:
Some things that fly there be, --
    Birds, hours, the bumble-bee:
    Of these no elegy.

    Some things that stay there be, --
    Grief, hills, eternity:
    Nor this behooveth me.

    There are, that resting, rise.
    Can I expound the skies?
    How still the riddle lies!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "The Secret.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
